message:
perf(runtime/scheduler): replace AtomicBool::swap with compare_exchange in Parker park/unpark

In the Parker implementation used by runtime scheduler workers, replace
AtomicBool::swap with compare_exchange for both the park and unpark
operations.

Previously, swap(false, Acquire) in park unconditionally wrote false
even when the value was already false, and swap(true, Release) in
unpark unconditionally wrote true even when already true. Each
unconditional write invalidates the cache line on all other cores that
hold a copy, causing false sharing overhead on multi-core systems where
multiple workers frequently park and unpark.

The new implementation uses:
- compare_exchange(true, false, Acquire, Relaxed) in park: only writes
  when transitioning from notified (true) to consumed (false).
- compare_exchange(false, true, Release, Relaxed) in unpark: only
  writes when transitioning from not-notified (false) to notified
  (true).

This eliminates unnecessary cache-line invalidation when the atomic
already holds the desired value, reducing inter-core coherence traffic
in the common case where workers are contending on park/unpark.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
